user_query:
I want a small, self-contained nutrition log for three specific days using only the provided references. Read these inputs: input/meals_2024-03-10_to_2024-03-12.json (meal schedule and items), input/foods_reference.csv (per-portion calories/macros and notable micronutrients for each exact item name), and input/targets.md (daily calorie and macro targets). Then:

1) Create daily markdown logs at these exact paths:
- output/nutrition/daily/2024-03/2024-03-10.md
- output/nutrition/daily/2024-03/2024-03-11.md
- output/nutrition/daily/2024-03/2024-03-12.md

Each file must follow this exact structure and formatting:
- First line: "# YYYY-MM-DD" (e.g., "# 2024-03-10").
- For each meal in chronological order, use a header: "## <Meal> — <HH:MM>" (Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner should include the given times; for Snacks use "## Snacks").
- Under each meal, list one line per item using these exact rules:
  "- <Food name>: <cal> cal, <protein>g protein, <carbs>g carbs, <fat>g fat"
  The numbers must come directly from input/foods_reference.csv for the exact item name (no substitutions or web lookups). Do not include thousands separators.
- After all meals, add a section:
  "## Daily Totals"
  "Calories: <integer>"
  "Protein: <number>g | Carbs: <number>g | Fat: <number>g"
  where calories are the integer sum of item calories for the day, and macros are the precise sums (include decimals if present). Totals must exactly equal the sum of the item lines in that file.
- Add a section:
  "## Micronutrients Notable"
  with bullet points listing the unique micronutrient keywords drawn from the 'micronutrients' column for all items consumed that day (e.g., Vitamin D, Omega-3, Vitamin C, Potassium, Iron, Calcium). Deduplicate the list.

2) Create a weekly summary JSON at output/nutrition/weekly/2024-03-10_to_2024-03-12.json with this schema:
{
  "targets": {"calories": <int>, "protein_g": <number>, "carbs_g": <number>, "fat_g": <number>},
  "daily_totals": {
    "2024-03-10": {"calories": <number>, "protein_g": <number>, "carbs_g": <number>, "fat_g": <number>},
    "2024-03-11": {...},
    "2024-03-12": {...}
  },
  "weekly_totals": {"calories": <number>, "protein_g": <number>, "carbs_g": <number>, "fat_g": <number>},
  "averages": {"calories": <number>, "protein_g": <number>, "carbs_g": <number>, "fat_g": <number>},
  "average_vs_targets": {"calories": <number>, "protein_g": <number>, "carbs_g": <number>, "fat_g": <number>}
}
- Fill "targets" from input/targets.md.
- Compute "daily_totals" from the three daily markdown files you generate (not by re-reading inputs).
- Compute "weekly_totals" as the sum of the three days.
- Compute "averages" as weekly_totals divided by 3 and round each value to one decimal place.
- Compute "average_vs_targets" as averages minus targets and round each to one decimal place.

Important:
- Use only the items exactly as named in input/meals_2024-03-10_to_2024-03-12.json and match macros exactly to input/foods_reference.csv. Do not invent or substitute items.
- Ensure the item lines, day totals, and the weekly JSON are internally consistent.
- Write all outputs only under output/… paths as specified.
